description: Create a content slide on safety and security with a three-column layout: two text columns covering conveyance security and personnel security, plus a right sidebar navigation, with a supporting image footer.

header.headline: Insights: Europe, Middle East, India and Africa

items.text: Conveyance security
items.sub: To ensure secure transportation of cargo, the applicant must maintain measures that protect conveyance integrity:

items.text: Conveyances used in the supply chain must be capable of being effectively secured.
Operators must be trained to maintain cargo and conveyance security.
Operators must report actual or suspicious incidents, with proper records maintained for Customs and the AEO team.
Potential concealment areas on conveyances must be regularly inspected.
Transporters must maintain logs or monitoring records to ensure conveyance integrity during transit.
Predefined transport routes must be identified and monitored; random route checks should be conducted.
Drivers must report route delays (weather, traffic, diversions) to dispatch.
Management must periodically and randomly verify that transport logs and monitoring procedures are being followed.
To prevent internal threats, the applicant must conduct appropriate staff screening:
Reasonable checks must be performed during recruitment to ensure candidates have no past security-related or Customs-related convictions.
Employees in sensitive roles must undergo periodic background verifications.
Employees must carry identification that uniquely links them to the organization.
Processes must exist to identify and record unauthorized persons, including visitor ID and sign-in procedures.
Identification and system access must be removed promptly when employment ends.
